messages.content:
1_ Translate the following legal text into colloquial Farsi 2_ Place the Persian and English text side by side in the table 3_ From the beginning to the end of the text, there should be an English sentence on the left side and a Persian sentence on the right side.
       4- Using legal language for Persian translation

          .Lesson Twenty
Word Study
deterrence / di'terǝns / the inhibition of criminal behaviour by fear of punishment; the action of preventing by fear.
The most frequently advanced and widely accepted argument in favour of capital punishment is deterrence.
Imprisonment is seen as an adequate penalty and acceptable means of social control or deterrence.
Deterrence can only be effective if people think that there is a reasonable
possibility that they will be caught.
double-edged/'dabl ed3d / having two cutting edges; having different elements or purposes; acting in two ways.
Businesses should be aware that certain GATT's benefits will constitute a double-edged sword.
Your suggestion is a double-edged one.
This provision has a double-edged meaning.
gravity / 'grævǝti / extreme importance and a source of concern or worry; seriousness.
I don't think you realise the gravity of the situation.
Punishment varies according to the gravity of the offence.
They stand accused of crimes of the utmost gravity.
indulge in'dɅld3 / to become involved in an activity, especially one that is illegal
or disapproved.
Most of the children easily indulge in begging.
Merchants who indulge in black market operations commit a crime.
There is no need to indulge in such criminal activities.

| English Text | Farsi Text |
| --- | --- |
| Lesson Twenty | درس بیستم |
| Word Study | مطالعه واژگان |
| deterrence /di'terǝns/ the inhibition of criminal behaviour by fear of punishment; the action of preventing by fear. | بازدارندگی /di'terǝns/ جلوگیری از رفتار جرمی به واسطه ترس از مجازات؛ عمل پیشگیری از طریق ترس. |
| The most frequently advanced and widely accepted argument in favour of capital punishment is deterrence. | دلیلی که بیشترین بسامد را در تایید مجازات اعدام داشته و به طور گسترده پذیرفته شده، بازدارندگی است. |
| Imprisonment is seen as an adequate penalty and acceptable means of social control or deterrence. | حبس به عنوان مجازات مناسب و وسیله‌ای پذیرفته شده برای کنترل اجتماعی یا بازدارندگی دیده می‌شود. |
| Deterrence can only be effective if people think that there is a reasonable possibility that they will be caught. | بازدارندگی تنها در صورتی مؤثر است که افراد تصور کنند احتمال منطقی وجود دارد که دستگیر شوند. |
| double-edged /'dabl ed3d/ having two cutting edges; having different elements or purposes; acting in two ways. | دو لبه /'dabl ed3d/ داشتن دو لبه بُرنده؛ داشتن عناصر یا اهداف متفاوت؛ دو گانه عمل کردن. |
| Businesses should be aware that certain GATT's benefits will constitute a double-edged sword. | کسب و کارها باید آگاه باشند که برخی منافع گات شمشیری دولبه خواهد بود. |
| Your suggestion is a double-edged one. | پیشنهاد شما یکی دو لبه است. |
| This provision has a double-edged meaning. | این مفاد دارای معنایی دو لبه است. |
| gravity / 'grævǝti / extreme importance and a source of concern or worry; seriousness. | جدیت / 'grævǝti / اهمیت شدید و منبعی از نگرانی یا دغدغه؛ جدی بودن. |
| I don't think you realise the gravity of the situation. | فکر نمی‌کنم شما جدیت وضعیت را درک کرده‌اید. |
| Punishment varies according to the gravity of the offence. | مجازات بر اساس جدیت جرم تغییر می‌کند. |
| They stand accused of crimes of the utmost gravity. | آن‌ها متهم به جرایمی با بالاترین درجه از جدیت هستند. |
| indulge /in'dʌldʒ/ to become involved in an activity, especially one that is illegal or disapproved. | اغماض کردن /in'dʌldʒ/ درگیر شدن در فعالیتی، به ویژه یکی که غیر قانونی یا ناپسند است. |
| Most of the children easily indulge in begging. | اکثر کودکان به راحتی به التماس کردن اغماض می‌کنند. |
| Merchants who indulge in black market operations commit a crime. | تجاری که در عملیات بازار سیاه اغماض می‌کنند، جرم را انجام داده‌اند. |
| There is no need to indulge in such criminal activities. | نیازی به اغماض کردن در چنین فعالیت‌های جرمی نیست. |

inflictin'flikt / impose; to make someone suffer something; to cause to be suffered.
The punishments inflicted on the children were too severe.
Capital punishment can be inflicted for exceptional crimes.
All fines shall be moderate and no cruel or unusual punishments shall be inflicted.
inhibitin'hibit / to hinder; to restrict or prevent a process or an action; to restrain; to prohibit from doing something.
Outdated policies inhibit economic growth.
In this country, there is no internal common market and much to inhibit private investment.
Cost is not an inhibiting factor in the company's plans.
publicise/ 'pablisaiz / to give the public information about something; to bring to the attention of the public; to advertise.
The offence has been widely publicised in the press.
The new crimes and their punishments are publicised.
The actual name of the offender has not yet publicised.
rehabilitation / ri:ǝbili'tern / restoration of a criminal to a normal life by appropriate training; re-establishment.
A rehabilitation centre is established for drug addicts.
Probation and parole are substitutes for imprisonment as methods of
rehabilitation.
Rehabilitation is encouraged for youthful offenders.
retribution/retrr'bju:fn / a repayment in the form of punishment; punishment that is considered to be morally right and fully deserved.
The victims seek revenge and retribution for the latest terrorist outrages.
The philosophy concerning retribution is that man is rewarded for his good deed and punished for his bad deed.
Pre-literate societies dealt with crime as a matter of personal offence and
retribution.
treat tri:t to act or behave in a certain way towards someone.
In early times, the tendency was to treat the alien as a criminal.

| English Text | Farsi Text |
| --- | --- |
| inflict /in'flikt/ impose; to make someone suffer something; to cause to be suffered. | وارد آوردن /in'flikt/ الزام کردن؛ باعث رنج کسی شدن؛ موجب رنج بردن شدن. |
| The punishments inflicted on the children were too severe. | مجازات‌هایی که بر کودکان وارد شد، بیش از حد سخت بود. |
| Capital punishment can be inflicted for exceptional crimes. | مجازات اعدام می‌تواند برای جرایم استثنایی وارد آورده شود. |
| All fines shall be moderate and no cruel or unusual punishments shall be inflicted. | تمامی جرایم باید معتدل باشند و هیچ مجازات سختگیرانه یا غیر عادی نباید وارد آورده شود. |
| inhibit /in'hibit/ to hinder; to restrict or prevent a process or an action; to restrain; to prohibit from doing something. | محدود کردن /in'hibit/ مانع شدن؛ محدود یا جلوگیری از فرآیند یا عملی؛ بازداشتن؛ منع کردن از انجام دادن کاری. |
| Outdated policies inhibit economic growth. | سیاست‌های منسوخ رشد اقتصادی را محدود می‌کنند. |
| In this country, there is no internal common market and much to inhibit private investment. | در این کشور، بازار مشترک داخلی وجود ندارد و عوامل زیادی سرمایه‌گذاری خصوصی را محدود می‌کنند. |
| Cost is not an inhibiting factor in the company's plans. | هزینه عامل بازدارنده‌ای در طرح‌های شرکت نیست. |
| publicise / 'pablisaiz / to give the public information about something; to bring to the attention of the public; to advertise. | اطلاع‌رسانی کردن / 'pablisaiz / اطلاعاتی به عموم مردم دادن؛ موضوعی را به توجه عموم رساندن؛ تبلیغ کردن. |
| The offence has been widely publicised in the press. | جرم به طور گسترده در مطبوعات اطلاع‌رسانی شده است. |
| The new crimes and their punishments are publicised. | جرایم جدید و مجازات‌های آنها اطلاع‌رسانی می‌شوند. |
| The actual name of the offender has not yet publicised. | نام واقعی مرتکب هنوز اطلاع‌رسانی نشده است. |
| rehabilitation /ri:ǝbili'teɪʃn/ restoration of a criminal to a normal life by appropriate training; re-establishment. | بازپروری /ri:ǝbili'teɪʃn/ بازگرداندن یک مجرم به زندگی عادی از طریق آموزش مناسب؛ بازسازی. |
| A rehabilitation centre is established for drug addicts. | یک مرکز بازپروری برای معتادان به مواد مخدر تأسیس شده است. |
| Probation and parole are substitutes for imprisonment as methods of rehabilitation. | آزادی مشروط و پارول جایگزین‌هایی برای زندان به عنوان روش‌های بازپروری هستند. |
| Rehabilitation is encouraged for youthful offenders. | بازپروری برای مجرمان جوان تشویق می‌شود. |
| retribution /retri'bju:ʃn/ a repayment in the form of punishment; punishment that is considered to be morally right and fully deserved. | قصاص /retri'bju:ʃn/ پرداخت در قالب مجازات؛ مجازاتی که از نظر اخلاقی درست و کاملاً سزاوار تلقی می‌شود. |
| The victims seek revenge and retribution for the latest terrorist outrages. | قربانیان به دنبال انتقام و قصاص برای آخرین جنایات تروریستی هستند. |
| The philosophy concerning retribution is that man is rewarded for his good deed and punished for his bad deed. | فلسفه مربوط به قصاص این است که انسان برای کار نیک خود پاداش و برای کار بد خود مجازات می‌شود. |
| Pre-literate societies dealt with crime as a matter of personal offence and retribution. | جوامع پیش از خواندن و نوشتن، جرم را به عنوان مسئله‌ای از تخلف شخصی و قصاص می‌دیدند. |
| treat /tri:t/ to act or behave in a certain way towards someone. | رفتار کردن /tri:t/ عمل یا رفتاری به شیوه‌ای خاص نسبت به کسی. |
| In early times, the tendency was to treat the alien as a criminal. | در زمان‌های اولیه، گرایش بر این بود که بیگانه را به عنوان یک مجرم رفتار کنند. |

Young offenders should be treated in a way different from adults.
It is now considered preferable to treat the convicted criminal in open institutions if possible.

| English Text | Farsi Text |
| --- | --- |
| Young offenders should be treated in a way different from adults. | مجرمین جوان باید به شیوه‌ای متفاوت از بزرگسالان رفتار شوند. |
| It is now considered preferable to treat the convicted criminal in open institutions if possible. | اکنون ترجیح داده می‌شود در صورت امکان، مجرم محکوم شده در مؤسسات باز رفتار شود. |

The Purposes of Sentences
The main objectives of sentencing have usually been presented as falling into three categories: retribution, deterrence, and rehabilitation. Retribution is straightforward punishment inflicted on the offender in response to the offence. It is perhaps the oldest type of sanction.
Until recently, it was generally accepted that the sentencing policy of the courts tended to be not retribution, but deterrence. Deterrence is a double-edged principle. It should, of course, deter offenders themselves from committing further crimes, but also, and very importantly, it has been seen by the judges as serving to inhibit the rest of the community from indulging in criminal behaviour. The message is, simply "if you do this, this what you can expect." A deterrent sentence must be publicised among the community, but how many of us have any idea of the sentence we might expect if we were caught for a crime.
The third principle which may underlie criminal sanctions is that of rehabilitation. Essentially the rehabilitative sentence is tied no to the offence and its gravity, but to the offender and his needs. The sentencing judge must make a choice in any given case as to whether to pass a sentence linked to the offence (retributive or deterrent) or an individualised rehabilitative sentence, designed to reform or treat the offender for an identified problem.

| English Text | Farsi Text |
| --- | --- |
| The Purposes of Sentences | اهداف مجازات‌ها |
| The main objectives of sentencing have usually been presented as falling into three categories: retribution, deterrence, and rehabilitation. | اهداف اصلی صدور حکم معمولاً در سه دسته قرار می‌گیرند: قصاص، بازدارندگی، و بازپروری. |
| Retribution is straightforward punishment inflicted on the offender in response to the offence. It is perhaps the oldest type of sanction. | قصاص، مجازات مستقیمی است که به عنوان پاسخ به جرم، به مرتکب وارد می‌شود. شاید قدیمی‌ترین نوع تحریم باشد. |
| Until recently, it was generally accepted that the sentencing policy of the courts tended to be not retribution, but deterrence. | تا اواخر، عموماً پذیرفته شده بود که سیاست صدور حکم دادگاه‌ها تمایلی نه به قصاص، بلکه به بازدارندگی داشت. |
| Deterrence is a double-edged principle. It should, of course, deter offenders themselves from committing further crimes, but also, and very importantly, it has been seen by the judges as serving to inhibit the rest of the community from indulging in criminal behaviour. | بازدارندگی یک اصل دولبه است. البته، باید مرتکبین را از ارتکاب جرایم بیشتر باز دارد، اما همچنین و به طور بسیار مهمی، قضات این را به عنوان وسیله‌ای برای جلوگیری از درگیر شدن بقیه جامعه در رفتارهای جرمی دیده‌اند. |
| The message is, simply "if you do this, this is what you can expect." | پیام ساده است: "اگر این کار را انجام دهید، این چیزی است که می‌توانید انتظار داشته باشید." |
| A deterrent sentence must be publicised among the community, but how many of us have any idea of the sentence we might expect if we were caught for a crime. | یک حکم بازدارنده باید در میان جامعه منتشر شود، اما چند نفر از ما ایده‌ای از حکمی که ممکن است در صورت دستگیری برای یک جرم انتظار داشته باشیم، داریم. |
| The third principle which may underlie criminal sanctions is that of rehabilitation. | سومین اصلی که ممکن است زیربنای تحریم‌های جنایی باشد، بازپروری است. |
| Essentially the rehabilitative sentence is tied not to the offence and its gravity, but to the offender and his needs. | اساساً، حکم بازپروری نه به جرم و شدت آن، بلکه به مرتکب و نیازهای او مربوط می‌شود. |
| The sentencing judge must make a choice in any given case as to whether to pass a sentence linked to the offence (retributive or deterrent) or an individualised rehabilitative sentence, designed to reform or treat the offender for an identified problem. | قاضی صادرکننده حکم باید در هر موردی تصمیم بگیرد که آیا حکمی مرتبط با جرم (قصاص یا بازدارنده) صادر کند یا حکم بازپروری فردی‌سازی شده‌ای که به منظور اصلاح یا درمان مرتکب برای مشکل شناسایی شده طراحی شده است. |

Lesson Twenty-One
Word Study
decorum/di'ko:rəm / polite and socially acceptable behaviour; proper behaviour. The judge's conduct of the trial was a model of decorum and impartiality. Decorum, in literature, refers to the quality of fitness or propriety as an element
in beauty.
An atmosphere of decorum and efficiency is called rules of order.
disorderly dis'ɔ:dǝli / violating; opposed to order; contrary to public order or morality; irregular.
He was arrested for being drunk and disorderly.
Crimes which are usually classified as misdemeanours include disorderly
conduct.
It is an offence if three or more persons gather together for an unlawful purpose or in a disorderly manner.
disturb dr'stз:b/ to break the peace, rest, or concentration.
The strike continued to disturb international air traffic.
An assembly is unlawful if it causes persons in the vicinity to fear that the assembly will disturb the peace.
You should keep the noise down, otherwise you may disturb the neighbours. drunk draŋk / feeling the effects of alcoholic drink; having had too much alcoholic drink.
To be drunk in public is a crime in Iran.
Drunk drivers should be heavily penalised.
A drunk driver killed two children.
fight fait to struggle against someone using physical force; to defeat; to take part
in a battle.

| English Text | Farsi Text |
| --- | --- |
| Lesson Twenty-One | درس بیست و یک |
| Word Study | مطالعه واژگان |
| decorum /di'ko:rəm/ polite and socially acceptable behaviour; proper behaviour. | آداب /di'ko:rəm/ رفتار مودبانه و پذیرفته شده اجتماعی؛ رفتار مناسب. |
| The judge's conduct of the trial was a model of decorum and impartiality. | رفتار قاضی در جریان دادرسی الگویی از آداب و بی‌طرفی بود. |
| Decorum, in literature, refers to the quality of fitness or propriety as an element in beauty. | آداب در ادبیات، به کیفیت مناسبت یا صحت به عنوان یک عنصر در زیبایی اشاره دارد. |
| An atmosphere of decorum and efficiency is called rules of order. | فضایی از آداب و کارایی، قوانین نظم نامیده می‌شود. |
| disorderly /dis'ɔ:dǝli/ violating; opposed to order; contrary to public order or morality; irregular. | بی‌نظم /dis'ɔ:dɚli/ نقض کننده؛ مخالف نظم؛ خلاف نظم عمومی یا اخلاق؛ نامنظم. |
| He was arrested for being drunk and disorderly. | او به دلیل مستی و بی‌نظمی دستگیر شد. |
| Crimes which are usually classified as misdemeanours include disorderly conduct. | جرائمی که معمولاً به عنوان تخلفات طبقه‌بندی می‌شوند، شامل رفتار بی‌نظم می‌شوند. |
| It is an offence if three or more persons gather together for an unlawful purpose or in a disorderly manner. | اگر سه نفر یا بیشتر برای یک هدف غیرقانونی یا به شکلی بی‌نظم جمع شوند، جرم محسوب می‌شود. |
| disturb /dr'stз:b/ to break the peace, rest, or concentration. | مختل کردن /dr'stз:b/ بر هم زدن آرامش، استراحت، یا تمرکز. |
| The strike continued to disturb international air traffic. | اعتصاب همچنان به مختل کردن ترافیک هوایی بین‌المللی ادامه داد. |
| An assembly is unlawful if it causes persons in the vicinity to fear that the assembly will disturb the peace. | تجمع غیرقانونی است اگر باعث شود افراد در اطراف از این بترسند که تجمع آرامش را مختل کند. |
| You should keep the noise down, otherwise you may disturb the neighbours. | باید سروصدا را کم کنید، در غیر این صورت ممکن است همسایگان را مختل کنید. |
| drunk /dræŋk/ feeling the effects of alcoholic drink; having had too much alcoholic drink. | مست /dræŋk/ تحت تأثیر نوشیدنی‌های الکلی قرار گرفتن؛ مصرف بیش از حد نوشیدنی‌های الکلی. |
| To be drunk in public is a crime in Iran. | در ایران، مستی در ملاء عام جرم است. |
| Drunk drivers should be heavily penalised. | رانندگان مست باید به شدت جریمه شوند. |
| A drunk driver killed two children. | یک راننده مست دو کودک را کشت. |
| fight /fait/ to struggle against someone using physical force; to defeat; to take part in a battle. | جنگیدن /fait/ مقابله با کسی با استفاده از نیروی فیزیکی؛ شکست دادن؛ شرکت در یک نبرد. |

We all have a part to play in the fight against crime.
Computers have proved invaluable in the fight against crime. The duty of the lawyer is to fight for the rights of his client.
indecent in'di:snt / offending against accepted moral standards; not decent; not proper; obscene.
That short skirt of hers is positively indecent.
Where indecent material is involved, the court may order trials to be conducted
in private.
The publication of sexually explicit or indecent matter is prohibited.
lawyer 'lo:jǝ(r)/ an attorney at law; a person trained and qualified in the law who / does legal work for other people; solicitor; barrister.
In order to make a crime appear less serious, the lawyer said her client had been deeply depressed.
The judge told the lawyer not to browbeat the witness.
The lawyer cross-examined the defence witness.
offensive / ǝ'fensiv / causing upset; highly annoying; aggressive; insulting.
She finds tobacco smoke offensive.
Some countries adopt the offensive behaviour as a tool of policy.
The plaintiff had to prove that the disclosure complained of is highly offensive to a reasonable person.
propriety/prǝ'praiǝti / good manner; proper behaviour; conformity to established standards of behaviour.
The way tourists dress sometimes offends local standards of propriety.
The appeal is usually limited to the propriety of the finding of guilty and the
legality of the sentence.
The company had acted in accordance with all legal proprieties.
protect prǝ'tekt / to keep safe from harm or injury; to guard or defend from
injury, danger, or annoyance.
Several witnesses have decided to tell a lie in order to protect the accused.
Car industry in Iran is protected against competition by levying heavy tariff on foreign cars imported into the country.
It is the business of the police to protect the community.

| English Text | Farsi Text |
| --- | --- |
| We all have a part to play in the fight against crime. | همه ما نقشی در نبرد با جرم و جنایت داریم. |
| Computers have proved invaluable in the fight against crime. | کامپیوترها در مبارزه با جنایت بسیار ارزشمند اثبات شده‌اند. |
| The duty of the lawyer is to fight for the rights of his client. | وظیفه وکیل مبارزه برای حقوق موکلش است. |
| indecent /in'di:snt/ offending against accepted moral standards; not decent; not proper; obscene. | ناپسند /in'di:snt/ مغایرت با استانداردهای اخلاقی پذیرفته شده؛ نامناسب؛ ناشایست؛ زننده. |
| That short skirt of hers is positively indecent. | آن دامن کوتاه او کاملاً ناپسند است. |
| Where indecent material is involved, the court may order trials to be conducted in private. | زمانی که مواد ناپسند دخالت داشته باشد، دادگاه ممکن است دستور دهد که محاکمات به صورت خصوصی برگزار شود. |
| The publication of sexually explicit or indecent matter is prohibited. | انتشار مطالب جنسی صریح یا ناپسند ممنوع است. |
| lawyer /'lo:jǝ(r)/ an attorney at law; a person trained and qualified in the law who does legal work for other people; solicitor; barrister. | وکیل /'lo:jǝ(r)/ یک وکیل دادگستری؛ شخصی که در قانون آموزش دیده و واجد شرایط است که کارهای حقوقی را برای دیگران انجام دهد؛ مشاور حقوقی؛ وکیل پایه یک دادگستری. |
| In order to make a crime appear less serious, the lawyer said her client had been deeply depressed. | برای کم اهمیت نشان دادن یک جرم، وکیل گفت که موکلش به شدت افسرده بوده است. |
| The judge told the lawyer not to browbeat the witness. | قاضی به وکیل گفت که شاهد را مورد فشار قرار ندهد. |
| The lawyer cross-examined the defence witness. | وکیل شاهد دفاع را مورد بازجویی متقابل قرار داد. |
| offensive /ǝ'fensiv/ causing upset; highly annoying; aggressive; insulting. | توهین‌آمیز /ǝ'fensiv/ باعث ناراحتی؛ بسیار آزار دهنده؛ تهاجمی؛ توهین‌آمیز. |
| She finds tobacco smoke offensive. | او دود توتون را توهین‌آمیز می‌یابد. |
| Some countries adopt the offensive behaviour as a tool of policy. | برخی از کشورها رفتار توهین‌آمیز را به عنوان ابزاری از سیاست برمی‌گزینند. |
| The plaintiff had to prove that the disclosure complained of is highly offensive to a reasonable person. | شاکی مجبور بود اثبات کند که افشای شکایت شده به شدت برای یک شخص منطقی توهین‌آمیز است. |
| propriety /prǝ'praiǝti/ good manner; proper behaviour; conformity to established standards of behaviour. | پسندیدگی /prǝ'praiǝti/ رفتار خوب؛ رفتار مناسب؛ تطابق با استانداردهای مشخص شده رفتار. |
| The way tourists dress sometimes offends local standards of propriety. | نحوه پوشش گردشگران گاهی استانداردهای محلی پسندیدگی را نقض می‌کند. |
| The appeal is usually limited to the propriety of the finding of guilty and the legality of the sentence. | اعتراض معمولاً به پسندیدگی یافتن مجرم و قانونی بودن حکم محدود است. |
| The company had acted in accordance with all legal proprieties. | شرکت طبق تمامی پسندیدگی‌های قانونی عمل کرده است. |
| protect /prǝ'tekt/ to keep safe from harm or injury; to guard or defend from injury, danger, or annoyance. | حفاظت کردن /prǝ'tekt/ نگه داشتن در امان از آسیب یا جراحت؛ محافظت یا دفاع از جراحت، خطر، یا آزار. |
| Several witnesses have decided to tell a lie in order to protect the accused. | چندین شاهد تصمیم گرفتند برای حفاظت از متهم دروغ بگویند. |
| Car industry in Iran is protected against competition by levying heavy tariff on foreign cars imported into the country. | صنعت خودرو در ایران با اعمال تعرفه سنگین بر خودروهای خارجی وارداتی از رقابت محافظت می‌شود. |
| It is the business of the police to protect the community. | وظیفه پلیس حفاظت از جامعه است. |

Offences Against the Public Order (1)
A person who, in a public place or a police station, behaves in a disorderly or offensive manner, fights with another person or uses offensive language is guilty of an offence and liable to a fine or imprisonment. It is also an offence for a person to disturb the public peace, although it is hard to imagine conduct that would disturb the public peace without also being disorderly behaviour.
It is an offence to use language that is indecent. Language is indecent if it is highly offensive to the recognised standard of common propriety. This offence is not designed for the special protection of those who are easily shocked. It is meant to protect the public in its use of public places against any substantial breach of decorum. It is also an offence to use indecent language in a private place if it can be heard in public places. The circumstances in which language is used may be important in deciding whether the language is indecent.
In some countries, it is no longer an offence to be drunk in a public place. The police may take a person who is drunk in a public place into custody and take the person home or to a police station or a sobering up centre. While at either place, the person must be given a reasonable opportunity to contact a lawyer, relative or friend. It is an offence for one person to supply methylated spirits, or a liquid containing methylated spirits, to another if the person who supplies it knows or suspects that the other person intends to drink it.

| It is also an offence for a person to disturb the public peace, although it is hard to imagine conduct that would disturb the public peace without also being disorderly behaviour. | همچنین اخلال در آرامش عمومی توسط شخصی نیز جرم محسوب می‌شود، هرچند تصور رفتاری که آرامش عمومی را بر هم بزند بدون اینکه رفتاری بی‌نظم باشد، دشوار است. |
| It is an offence to use language that is indecent. Language is indecent if it is highly offensive to the recognised standard of common propriety. | استفاده از زبان ناپسند نیز جرم است. زبان ناپسند زمانی است که بسیار توهین‌آمیز به استاندارد شناخته شده‌ی پسندیدگی عمومی باشد. |
| This offence is not designed for the special protection of those who are easily shocked. It is meant to protect the public in its use of public places against any substantial breach of decorum. | این جرم برای حفاظت ویژه افرادی که به راحتی شوکه می‌شوند طراحی نشده است. این به منظور حفاظت از عموم در استفاده از مکان‌های عمومی در برابر هرگونه نقض قابل توجهی از آداب است. |
| It is also an offence to use indecent language in a private place if it can be heard in public places. | استفاده از زبان ناپسند در مکان‌های خصوصی نیز جرم است اگر در مکان‌های عمومی قابل شنیدن باشد. |
| The circumstances in which language is used may be important in deciding whether the language is indecent. | شرایطی که در آن زبان استفاده می‌شود ممکن است در تصمیم‌گیری درباره اینکه آیا زبان ناپسند است مهم باشد. |
| In some countries, it is no longer an offence to be drunk in a public place. | در برخی کشورها، دیگر جرم نیست که شخصی در مکان عمومی مست باشد. |
| The police may take a person who is drunk in a public place into custody and take the person home or to a police station or a sobering up centre. | پلیس ممکن است شخصی که در مکان عمومی مست است را بازداشت کند و او را به خانه یا کلانتری یا مرکز ترک الکل ببرد. |
| While at either place, the person must be given a reasonable opportunity to contact a lawyer, relative or friend. | در هر یک از این مکان‌ها، باید فرصت مناسبی به شخص داده شود تا با وکیل، خویشاوند یا دوست تماس بگیرد. |
| It is an offence for one person to supply methylated spirits, or a liquid containing methylated spirits, to another if the person who supplies it knows or suspects that the other person intends to drink it. | تامین الکل دناتوره شده، یا مایعی که حاوی الکل دناتوره شده باشد، توسط یک شخص به شخص دیگر جرم است اگر شخص تامین‌کننده بداند یا مظنون باشد که شخص دیگر قصد دارد آن را بنوشد. |

Lesson Twenty-Two
Word Study
beg/beg / to ask for money, food, or clothes as a gift or as charity.
There are hundreds begging in the streets.
He was so poor he had to beg for money from passers-by.
To get the children to beg is a crime.
bet betto risk money on a race or on some other event of which the result is
doubtful; wager.
I do not approve of betting.
He spends all his money betting on horses.
Although betting is generally illegal in Iran, it is legal to bet on horses as an
exception.
cheat/tfi:t/to trick or deceive someone.
He has been cheating the taxman for years.
Cheating is possible and reasonably easy in most gambling games.
Two players were disqualified for cheating.
confiscation/konfi'skerfn / the seizure and appropriation of property as a punishment; taking someone's property as a punishment.
If you are caught smuggling goods into the country, you will face the confiscation of the goods.
The confiscation of property, loss of citizenship, and loss of civil rights were some sorts of punishment.
A smuggler could be punished by the confiscation of property and life imprisonment.
